purpose: Turn existing client surface skeletons and foundation services into real runnable TUI, Neovim/Vim and Eclipse client implementations, with tests, smoke checks and truthful completion evidence.

known_runtime_gaps: TUI skeleton directory exists but no runnable TUI app entrypoint is proven.
Neovim skeleton directories exist but no real Lua plugin modules or command registration are proven.
Vim compatibility skeleton directories exist but no real Vim plugin/autoload files are proven.
Eclipse foundation service exists but no Eclipse plugin project metadata/build is proven.
Runtime inventory still lists runtime evidence arrays as empty.
data/client_surface_runtime_inventory.json still points to previous track id and must be refreshed for this track.
scripts/audit_client_surface_entrypoints.py still maps done-claim ranges from old CSH/TVM/ECL IDs and must be aligned to CRT IDs.

goal: At the end of this track, Ananta must contain real runnable client surfaces or explicitly truthful downgraded status: a TUI runtime, a Neovim plugin runtime, a clear Vim compatibility status, and a buildable Eclipse plugin MVP or documented blocked status with evidence.
success_criteria: TUI has a real start command and at least health, task and artifact views.
Neovim plugin has real runtime files and user commands that can be loaded in headless Neovim.
Vim compatibility is either implemented with real plugin/autoload files or explicitly marked deferred/unsupported.
Eclipse plugin has real plugin project metadata and a documented build or is explicitly marked blocked with concrete reason.
Runtime inventory is updated from foundation_only to runtime_mvp only when executable evidence exists.
Audit script fails on .keep-only client surfaces when runtime_mvp/runtime_complete is claimed.
Unit, integration and smoke checks prove client startup, backend connection handling, degraded states and core actions.
Docs show only commands that actually work or are explicitly marked planned.
architecture_guardrails: Clients remain thin adapters; hub orchestration, planning, routing, policy, approval and audit stay in Ananta.
No client may silently mutate files.
No client may bypass approval, policy, verification or audit paths.
All editor/TUI context sent to Ananta must be bounded and visible.
Skeleton directories and docs alone never satisfy runtime completion.
Runtime evidence must include files, commands and tests.

milestones.title: Shared client API and context layer
Eclipse plugin MVP or explicit blocker

tasks.title: Runtime inventory update rules harden
acceptance_criteria: data/client_surface_runtime_inventory.json keeps all .keep-only surfaces classified as foundation_only.
A surface may become runtime_mvp only if runtime evidence includes real non-.keep files, start/load command and smoke evidence.
Inventory evidence must list exact runtime file paths for TUI, Neovim/Vim and Eclipse.

tasks.title: Audit script fail on skeleton-only runtime claims
acceptance_criteria: scripts/audit_client_surface_entrypoints.py treats .keep files as no runtime evidence.
DONE_CLAIM_RULES includes current CRT task ranges so done runtime tasks are actually checked.
Audit fails if TUI runtime claim tasks are done while classification is not real_implementation.
Audit fails if Neovim/Vim runtime claim tasks are done while classification is not real_implementation.
Audit fails if Eclipse runtime claim tasks are done while classification is not real_implementation.

tasks.title: Completion definitions align with audit
acceptance_criteria: data/client_surface_completion_definitions.json defines completion labels consistently with audit output classes.
Definitions explicitly say skeleton directories do not count as runtime.
Definitions include mapping from runtime_mvp/runtime_complete to audit evidence checks.
Definitions include required evidence for each client surface.

tasks.title: Claim reconciliation refresh
acceptance_criteria: data/client_surface_claim_reconciliation.json is updated after implementation work.
It states which old todo claims were foundation-only and which became runtime_mvp.
No old Eclipse/TUI/Vim claim remains ambiguous.

tasks.title: CRT done-claim mapping in audit script align
acceptance_criteria: scripts/audit_client_surface_entrypoints.py DONE_CLAIM_RULES include CRT task ranges for TUI, Neovim/Vim and Eclipse runtime claims.
Audit output lists done_claims for CRT tasks once such tasks are marked done.
Tests cover the new CRT mapping behavior.

tasks.title: Shared client API wrapper implement
acceptance_criteria: A reusable client layer exists for health, capabilities, goals, tasks, artifacts and approvals.
TUI and editor/plugin clients can use the same request/response semantics.
Wrapper has timeouts and typed degraded-state results.
Wrapper does not contain planning, routing, worker or policy logic.

tasks.title: Client profile and auth handling implement
acceptance_criteria: Clients can configure Ananta base URL and auth/profile data.
Secrets are not logged, echoed or persisted unsafely.
Auth failure is surfaced as explicit degraded state.
Tests cover missing token, invalid token and backend unavailable.

tasks.title: Bounded context packaging implement
acceptance_criteria: Editor/TUI context payloads include provenance and size metadata.
Selection, file path and project metadata are bounded.
No unrelated workspace files are included implicitly.
Likely secret patterns are blocked, redacted or warned before send.

tasks.title: Client degraded-state model implement
acceptance_criteria: Clients distinguish backend_unreachable, auth_failed, capability_missing, policy_denied, malformed_response and unknown_error.
Unsupported or blocked actions are visibly blocked.
No client reports success when backend operation failed.
Unit tests cover every degraded state.

tasks.title: TUI runtime entrypoint implement
acceptance_criteria: client_surfaces/tui_runtime/ananta_tui contains real Python runtime files, not only .keep.
A documented command starts the TUI, for example python -m client_surfaces.tui_runtime.ananta_tui or ananta tui.
Startup works with configured or mocked backend.
Startup failure produces a readable error instead of traceback-only behavior.

tasks.title: TUI health and connection view implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI shows current endpoint/profile and backend health.
Connected, disconnected and auth-failed states render differently.
View works in realistic terminal sizes.
Tests cover healthy and degraded startup.

tasks.title: TUI task and artifact views implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI can list tasks from backend or test fixture.
TUI can show task detail with status, summary and related artifact references.
TUI can list artifacts and show text-heavy artifact detail.
Empty and malformed data states are handled.

tasks.title: TUI approval and repair visibility implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI can display approval-required and policy-denied states.
Approval/reject action, if implemented, calls backend approval API only.
Repair sessions show diagnosis, proposed steps, verification and outcome where backend data exists.
No repair action is executed implicitly from view rendering.

tasks.title: TUI tests and smoke checks add
acceptance_criteria: TUI view-model tests cover health, task, artifact, empty and error states.
A smoke check starts the TUI or a deterministic scripted mode.
Smoke check verifies at least health plus task/artifact rendering.
Audit script recognizes TUI as runtime_mvp only after this passes.

tasks.title: Neovim runtime files implement
acceptance_criteria: client_surfaces/nvim_runtime/lua/ananta contains real Lua modules, not only .keep.
client_surfaces/nvim_runtime/plugin contains a real plugin entrypoint file.
Plugin loads in headless Neovim without runtime errors.
Runtime inventory lists exact Lua/plugin runtime files.

tasks.title: Neovim Ananta commands implement
acceptance_criteria: Plugin exposes commands for goal submit, analyze and review.
Commands call Ananta backend goal/task flows via client layer.
Command output renders success, failure and degraded states clearly.
Commands do not mutate project files.

tasks.title: Neovim bounded editor context implement
acceptance_criteria: Plugin can capture current file path and bounded buffer excerpt.
Plugin can capture visual selection where supported.
User can inspect or confirm context before send.
No unrelated project files are sent implicitly.

tasks.title: Neovim result rendering implement
acceptance_criteria: Results render in scratch buffer, split or floating window.
Task/artifact identifiers or browser links are visible.
Unsupported artifact types degrade to safe text/browser fallback.
Rendering tests or smoke script cover at least one successful result.

tasks.title: Vim compatibility status decide and enforce
acceptance_criteria: Project explicitly decides whether first runtime is Neovim-only or Vim-compatible.
If Vim-compatible is claimed, client_surfaces/vim_compat contains real plugin/autoload files.
If Vim is deferred, runtime inventory and docs mark Vim as deferred rather than runtime_mvp.
Audit script enforces the chosen status.

tasks.title: Neovim/Vim smoke checks add
acceptance_criteria: Headless Neovim smoke check loads the plugin and lists/registers Ananta commands.
Smoke check exercises at least one mocked command path.
If Vim support is claimed, a Vim smoke check or manual checklist exists.
Audit script recognizes Neovim/Vim runtime only after smoke evidence exists.

tasks.title: Eclipse plugin runtime decision refresh
acceptance_criteria: Decision states whether this track will implement an actual Eclipse plugin MVP now or mark Eclipse runtime as blocked/deferred.
Decision references expected plugin layout and build tool if implemented.
If deferred, inventory remains foundation_only and docs stop claiming runtime availability.

tasks.title: Eclipse plugin project skeleton implement if not deferred
acceptance_criteria: If Eclipse runtime is pursued, repository contains a real Eclipse plugin project directory.
Project includes plugin.xml and META-INF/MANIFEST.MF or equivalent PDE/Tycho/Gradle metadata.
Project has stable plugin id and package namespace.
If not pursued, this task records explicit blocked/deferred status with reason.

tasks.title: Eclipse build and connection MVP implement if not deferred
acceptance_criteria: Plugin build command is documented and works locally or in CI.
Plugin can configure Ananta endpoint/profile.
Plugin can call health/capability endpoint.
Build failure blocks runtime_mvp.

tasks.title: Eclipse context handoff and commands implement if not deferred
acceptance_criteria: Plugin can collect current selection/file/project metadata.
Plugin exposes at least analyze/review/custom goal commands.
Context is bounded and visible before send.
Commands call Ananta backend APIs only.

tasks.title: Eclipse task/artifact view and smoke checklist add if not deferred
acceptance_criteria: Plugin displays returned tasks or artifacts in an Eclipse view.
Open-in-browser fallback exists.
Manual checklist covers install/open, configure backend, send selection, create goal and inspect task/artifact.
Runtime inventory remains foundation_only if these checks are not available.

tasks.title: External client API contract tests add
acceptance_criteria: Contract tests cover fields used by TUI, Neovim/Vim and Eclipse surfaces.
Tests cover health, capabilities, goals, tasks, artifacts, approvals and errors.
Breaking client-facing API changes fail tests.
Tests include degraded-state response shapes.

tasks.title: Client-originated security tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify client-originated actions cannot bypass approval or policy.
Tests verify tokens/secrets are redacted in client-facing logs/errors.
Tests cover malformed, stale, denied and unauthorized client requests.
Tests cover context payload size and provenance constraints.

tasks.title: Client golden path e2e smoke add
acceptance_criteria: At least one client path submits a goal and observes task/artifact result with mocked worker/LLM execution.
TUI and Neovim smoke paths are included if runtime_mvp is claimed.
Eclipse smoke/manual path is included if Eclipse runtime_mvp is claimed.
Smoke evidence is referenced in runtime inventory.

tasks.title: Release gate update for client surfaces
acceptance_criteria: Release gate runs audit_client_surface_entrypoints.py.
Release gate blocks runtime_mvp/runtime_complete claims without executable evidence.
Release gate reports each surface status: foundation_only, runtime_mvp, runtime_complete, deferred or blocked.
Gate output is documented for main merge review.

tasks.title: Documentation truth pass perform
acceptance_criteria: README and docs only claim runnable clients that actually exist.
TUI, Neovim/Vim and Eclipse docs include executable commands or explicit planned/deferred labels.
Docs explain foundation_only versus runtime_mvp.
No doc says Eclipse/TUI/Vim is complete based only on foundation services.

tasks.title: Merge readiness report generate
acceptance_criteria: Report lists final status for TUI, Neovim, Vim and Eclipse.
Report includes runtime file evidence, commands and smoke/test results.
Report separates blockers for main from later enhancements.
Report references updated inventory and audit results.

tasks.title: Todo summary and stage consistency guard add
acceptance_criteria: A validation command or script verifies tasks_status_summary and execution_stage_summary against real task data.
Guard is run before marking large task blocks done in this track.
Track summary counters cannot silently drift from task reality.

execution_order_summary: Stage 1: Harden audit/inventory so skeleton-only cannot pass.
Stage 2: Implement shared client API, auth, context and degraded states.
Stage 3: Fill TUI skeleton with real runtime app and smoke tests.
Stage 4: Fill Neovim skeleton with real plugin runtime; decide Vim compatibility truthfully.
Stage 5: Either implement Eclipse plugin MVP or explicitly mark Eclipse runtime deferred/blocked.
Stage 7: Update release gate, docs, inventory and merge readiness report.

alignment_issues_found: TUI runtime MVP is now executable and tested; Neovim/Eclipse remain foundation-only and Vim remains deferred via surface status policy.

summary_notes: Completed block: CRT-T01..CRT-T13 plus CRT-T18 and CRT-T31 (TUI runtime MVP + Vim status enforcement + audit/definitions alignment).
TUI runtime now has executable entrypoint, shared client layer, bounded context, degraded-state handling, tests and smoke script.
Vim compatibility is explicitly deferred and enforced via runtime status + audit checks.
This todo assumes skeleton directories and audit/inventory files already exist.
The main remaining work is real runtime implementation and executable evidence.
TUI and Neovim should be implemented as runtime MVPs in this track.
Eclipse may either become a real buildable plugin MVP or remain explicitly deferred/blocked, but must not be claimed complete without plugin metadata and build evidence.
This todo was re-aligned against real code evidence from scripts/audit_client_surface_entrypoints.py and existing runtime/foundation artifacts.
